nodes.title: Render the fixed board with cell identity
nodes.why: The nine board positions are fixed, so their index is the stable identity used for clicks and labels.
nodes.alternative: Recreate untracked buttons with no connection to board positions.
nodes.tradeoff: Index tracking is correct for this fixed grid, but movable collections should use domain ids.

nodes.title: Stop moves after game completion
nodes.why: The component rejects further state changes after a win or draw even if play is called programmatically.
nodes.alternative: Depend only on disabled cell buttons in the template.
nodes.tradeoff: The guard duplicates UI protection, but it preserves the game invariant for every caller.

nodes.title: Create the next board state
nodes.why: A copied board makes each move a clear state transition before winner detection.
nodes.alternative: Mutate the existing board array in place and rely on incidental change detection.
nodes.tradeoff: Copying nine cells is negligible and makes state transitions easier to inspect.

nodes.title: Compare every winning triplet

anchor.snippet: if (v && v === b[c] && v === b[d]) return v;

nodes.why: A non-null mark that matches both remaining positions identifies the winner for that line.
nodes.alternative: Hard-code winner checks in the play method for each possible move.
nodes.tradeoff: Iterating a line table is compact and testable, while custom board sizes would need generated combinations.
